channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I got fed up with my city’s bike-share, so I built BiziData
item.description: Like many of you, I use bike-share system pretty regularly. And, honestly, it drives me nuts: either there’s not a single bike when I need one, or the station is packed and I can’t return mine. It...

item.title: Why Your RAG System Needs a Graph Database (Not Just Vectors)
item.description: Why Your RAG System Needs a Graph Database (Not Just Vectors) The standard RAG stack is: embed your documents, store them in a vector database, retrieve the top-k most similar chunks at query...

item.title: Building Conversational AI: Memory Patterns, Context Management, and Conversation Design
item.description: Chatbots are easy to build. Conversational AI that actually works is hard. The difference? State management. A real conversation requires remembering what was said, managing context limits, and...

item.title: Reliable AI Outputs: Function Calling, JSON Mode, and Structured Generation in C#
item.description: LLMs are impressive text generators, but production applications need more than prose. You need structured data—JSON objects you can deserialize, decisions you can act on, and outputs that fit...

item.title: I was spending an hour every week on spreadsheets to figure out if my apps were profitable - so I automated it
item.description: Every week I'd open four different dashboards: Google Play Console → revenue from Android apps App Store Connect → revenue from iOS apps AdMob → ad earnings Google Ads → ad spent on android user...

item.title: Bringing Chrome to ARM64 Linux Devices
item.description: Article URL: https://blog.chromium.org/2026/03/bringing-chrome-to-arm64-linux-devices.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47356392 Points: 6 # Comments: 1

item.title: 95% of Nostr Relays Can't Cover Costs. Here's Why That Matters.
item.description: Running on Faith You run a relay because you believe in something. Censorship resistance. Permissionless publishing. A social protocol that nobody controls. You set it up on a VPS, configured the...

item.title: Remote Teams Are Spending 40% More on AI Tools—Here's What Actually Works
item.description: The meeting summarizer space is crowded. The async communication market is flooded. Project management tools now all claim AI. And remote teams are drowning in options. Here's what's actually...

item.title: Your AI Agent Just Sent PHI Through a Third-Party Server
item.description: A hospital deploys an AI agent to summarize patient records. The agent sends those summaries to a specialist's AI assistant for review. The communication crosses a network boundary. The summaries...

item.title: Write One Query Class Instead of Many: The AQC Design Pattern
item.description: When I introduced the Atomic Query Construction (AQC) design pattern, a few people asked: "Is this something new? Or is it just a renamed version of something that already exists?" Fair question....

item.title: iPhones and iPads Approved for NATO Classified Data
item.description: Apple announcement: …iPhone and iPad are the first and only consumer devices in compliance with the information assurance requirements of NATO nations. This enables iPhone and iPad to be used with...

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Anthropic’s DOD Lawsuit, War Memes, and AI Coming for VC Jobs
item.description: In today’s episode, we discuss how the saga between Anthropic and the Department of Defense is far from over.

item.title: What it was like to watch grieving parents stare down Mark Zuckerberg in court
item.description: Around a dozen parents huddled in the dim hallway outside the courtroom in February, nervously gripping paper tickets. They were glaring at a gray tote bag held by a member of the court staff -...

item.title: The original AirTag is the cheapest it’s ever been
item.description: Despite the fact Apple released a new AirTag in January, the first-generation AirTag is still a top-notch tracker if you’re embedded in Apple’s ecosystem. And right now, it’s on sale for $13.91...

item.title: KPop Demon Hunters is getting a sequel, obviously
item.description: Get ready to get even more K-pop, demonier, and as huntery as possible. Netflix's smash-hit, Oscar-nominated animated film KPop Demon Hunters is returning for a sequel, with the fictional Korean...

item.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
item.description: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup...

item.title: Google Is Not Ruling Out Ads in Gemini
item.description: WIRED spoke with Nick Fox, Google’s SVP of knowledge and information, about how AI is changing the company’s advertising business.

item.title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
item.description: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to re-engange its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
item.description: Facebook Marketplace is adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically...

item.title: Rust-Based VENON Malware Targets 33 Brazilian Banks with Credential-Stealing Overlays
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new banking malware targeting Brazilian users that's written in Rust, marking a significant departure from other known Delphi-based malware...

item.title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
item.description: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

item.title: Hive0163 Uses AI-Assisted Slopoly Malware for Persistent Access in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a suspected artificial intelligence (AI)-generated malware codenamed Slopoly put to use by a financially motivated threat actor named Hive0163....

item.title: Gemini’s task automation is here and it’s wild
item.description: A couple of weeks ago, Google and Samsung announced a big Gemini development coming to their newest devices: task automation. Starting with food delivery and rideshare apps, Gemini would be able...

item.title: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
item.description: Bumble's new AI assistant Bee will move the dating app beyond the swipe by matching people based on compatibility and goals.

item.title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
item.description: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for violating her privacy and publicity rights.

item.title: Google’s TV Streamer 4K doubles as a smart home hub and it’s on sale
item.description: Between March Madness and the Oscars, now might be a good time to improve your TV setup. Google’s TV Streamer 4K is currently on sale for $79.99 ($20 off) at Amazon, Target, and Walmart, which is...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude AI can respond with charts, diagrams, and other visuals now
item.description: Anthropic's latest update to Claude will allow the AI chatbot to generate custom charts, diagrams, and other visualizations during your conversation. If Claude determines a visual is useful based...

item.title: Gumloop lands $50M from Benchmark to turn every employee into an AI agent builder
item.description: As companies race to adopt AI, Benchmark general partner Everett Randle believes the key to success lies in empowering every worker with AI superpowers, and Gumloop’s intuitive agent builder is an...

item.title: Meta exec hopes VR teens will stick around
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. "It is a pretty rough time for...

item.title: Microsoft’s head of experiences and devices is retiring, triggering a shakeup
item.description: Rajesh Jha, executive vice president of Microsoft's experiences and devices group, is retiring after more than 35 years at the software giant. Jha was a big part of the team that helped move...
